response:
Sec. 1  
[THE WRITER HAS TWO PUBLISHER OFFERS TO CONSIDER BASED ON SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 2  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 3000 DOLLARS PLUS 5 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 1500 DOLLARS PLUS 10 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 4  
[TO FIND WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE, WE SET UP AN INEQUALITY COMPARING THE TWO OFFERS.]  

Sec. 5  
[THE INEQUALITY IS 3000 PLUS 0.05X GREATER THAN 1500 PLUS 0.10X.]  

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY GIVES THE RANGE OF X WHERE PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL.]  

Sec. 7  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED SALES REVENUE OF 10000 TO 40000 DOLLARS, THE RANGE IS FROM 30000 TO 40000 DOLLARS.]  

Sec. 8  
[THUS, PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL WHEN SALES REVENUE IS BETWEEN 30000 AND 40000 DOLLARS.]
